docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Welcome to Dendron
docs.text: This is the root of your dendron vault. If you decide to publish your entire vault, this will be your landing page. You are free to customize any part of this page except the frontmatter on top.

docs.title: Lookup
docs.text:
This section contains useful links to related resources.

  • Getting Started Guide

docs.title: 🧩 Docker Compose Mastery: The 80/20 Playbook
docs.text: Learn Compose not as a config file, but as a deployment weapon.

docs.title: 🔥 Phase 1: The Must-Know Concepts
docs.text:
Focus on mastering these power levers:

Topic 20% That Matters Multi-container apps Compose = instant orchestration depends_on Set app startup order (but not readiness!) Volumes Mount code/data across restarts Networks Internal container DNS, avoids localhost Service configs build, image, environment, command Deploy override Use .env + docker-compose.override.yml

docs.title: 🧪 Phase 2: Hands-On Tactical Challenges
docs.text: Each challenge teaches multiple core Compose skills.

docs.title: ✅ compose.01.basic-two-service
docs.text:
Goal: Connect a Node.js API to Postgres

version: "3.8"
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      - DB_HOST=db
      - DB_PORT=5432

  db:
    image: postgres:14
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret

docs.title: 🧩 compose.02.bind-mount-dev-sync
docs.text:
Goal: Sync local code changes into container

volumes:
  - .:/usr/src/app

Great for local dev with hot-reload. Use with Node, Flask, Django, etc.

docs.title: 🔐 compose.03.env-secrets-config
docs.text:
Goal: Inject secrets securely using .env

POSTGRES_PASSWORD=topsecret

env_file:
  - .env

Pro move: Use Git-ignored .env and inject config only at runtime.

docs.title: 🚀 compose.04.override-strategy
docs.text:
Goal: Use multiple Compose files for env-specific setup

docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up

Use this to change DBs, images, or configs between dev/prod.

docs.title: 🧠 Phase 3: Mental Models
docs.text:
• Services ≠ Containers — think of each service as a “role” that spawns containers.
  • Networks are magic — every service can reach the other by its name, like api or redis.
  • Docker Compose is declarative — it reconciles the state on your behalf.
  • Use Compose for everything — local dev, CI/CD, smoke testing, even infra bootstrap.

docs.title: 💡 Secrets Management
docs.text: Use env_file, GitHub Actions secrets, or Vault for sensitive values.

docs.title: ⚙️ Deployment Command Pattern
docs.text:
docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build

🔥 Pro tip: Always --build on first prod deploy or after config change.

docs.title: 📜 Phase 5: Cheat Commands You Must Memorize
docs.text:
docker-compose up -d         # Start everything
docker-compose down          # Stop & clean up
docker-compose build         # Build images
docker-compose logs -f       # Real-time logs
docker-compose exec app bash # Shell into container
docker-compose ps            # Check running services
